title: Define and monitor pipeline SLAs
description: Attach explicit completion-time SLAs to critical tasks so a late pipeline pages someone instead of silently slipping.
body: Use when downstream consumers depend on a pipeline finishing by a deadline. Do not rely on someone noticing a stale dashboard — a slipping pipeline should announce itself. Actions: set an SLA on the critical task or DAG, emit a notification when the deadline is missed, and track run duration trends to catch creeping slowness before it breaches. Validation signals include an SLA-miss alert firing on a deliberately delayed run and a duration trend exposing gradual slowdown. Risk boundary: an unmonitored late pipeline feeds stale data to decisions; if a critical task has no SLA, treat the gap as an operational risk and escalate to define one before consumers depend on its timeliness.
